item.title: Full Interview: Dave Portnoy
item.description: Founder and president of Barstool Sports, Dave Portnoy, sat down with "CBS Saturday Morning" co-host Kelly O'Grady to discuss his new book, "Cancel Me If You Can." He describes how he is understood, or misunderstood, in the public eye, what his motivations are and more.

item.title: 10 injured in overnight Russian strikes on Kyiv
item.description: Ukrainian military said its air defenses shot down or suppressed two missiles and 111 drones, while Russia claims its forces targeted drone production facilities in Kyiv.

item.title: Journalists reportedly issued subpoenas over Air Force One reporting
item.description: The subpoenas were issued after the New York Times reported on alleged security concerns with the new Qatari-gifted Air Force One.

item.title: U.S. and Iran to continue talks amid heightened tensions
item.description: U.S. and Iranian delegations are expected to continue negotiations in Oman via mediators after a week of intense attacks between both sides.

item.title: Police investigating murder of former British politician found dead in her home
item.description: Ann Widdecombe, a former member of Parliament, had "sustained serious injuries" when she was found dead in her home in southwest England, police said.

item.title: Man picking up debris loses hand after blast on California beach, family says
item.description: The family says the man was walking along the beach picking up discarded fireworks and other trash when he picked up what they described as a "bomb-like device" that unexpectedly exploded.

item.title: Bipartisan housing bill becomes law after Trump refuses to sign it
item.description: A landmark housing bill automatically became law overnight after President Trump declined to sign it.

item.title: Gordie Howe Bridge connecting Detroit, Canada expected to open in late July
item.description: The 1.5-mile-long bridge, which spans the Detroit River and connects the Motor City with Windsor, Ontario, is expected to open on July 27.

item.title: Arthur Fery's Cinderella run ends in Wimbledon semifinal match to Alexander Zverev
item.description: Championship weekend at Wimbledon has arrived, but it will not include the "Fery-tale" British matchup that tennis fans had been hoping for. Matthew Futterman, senior writer for tennis at The Athletic, joins "The Daily Report" to preview the gentlemen's singles final.

item.title: Judge vacates convictions of 4 Proud Boys in Jan. 6 Capitol insurrection
item.description: A federal judge on Friday agreed to dismiss the convictions of four members of the far-right Proud Boys group for their actions in the Jan. 6 Capitol riot.

item.title: How Congress' housing bill may, or may not, impact Americans
item.description: A sweeping bipartisan housing affordability bill is set to go into law at midnight on Friday, without President Trump's signature. The president says his decision not to sign the bill is a protest against lawmakers' failure to pass his package of voting reforms and restrictions. Bruce Marks, founder and CEO of the Neighborhood Assistance Corporation of America, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

item.title: Former CIA officer breaks down the latest with Iran
item.description: Senior U.S. officials say Iran privately told mediators that it made a mistake in shooting at commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz. The officials say Iran blamed the attacks on hardliners who are trying to undermine negotiations. Elliot Ackerman, a CBS News contributor and a former CIA officer, has more.

item.title: "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" guests for July 12, 2026
item.description: This week's guests include White House border czar Tom Homan and retired Gen. Frank McKenzie, president of The Citadel and former commander of U.S. Central Command.

item.title: Hegseth frustrated with lack of adherence to grooming rules
item.description: Eight months ago, Hegseth told top military leaders there would be "no more beardos" and "fat troops."

item.title: A boy's simple habit of waving to his neighbors transformed his community
item.description: Roman Butzlaff brought together a group of neighbors who say they would have barely known each other if not for a little boy, who lived in a neighborhood but needed a village.

item.title: What stands out from latest Pentagon UFO files release, according to Disclosure Foundation director
item.description: The Pentagon on Friday released a new batch of UFO files, spanning 19 videos and more. Jordan Flowers, executive director of the Disclosure Foundation, joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: Graham Platner formally drops out of Maine Senate race
item.description: Graham Platner had until 5 p.m. on Monday to formally suspend his campaign or he would remain on the ballot in November.

item.title: 4-year-old heals "inner loneliness" by bringing his neighborhood together
item.description: The old adage "it takes a village to raise a child" was turned on its head when it took a child to unite a village in North Carolina. Steve Hartman goes "On the Road" with the story.

item.title: Detainees say ICE officer shot Houston driver through passenger window, lawyer says
item.description: Three men who witnessed a fatal shooting involving federal immigration officers in Houston say no officer was threatened, a lawyer who has spoken with them said.

item.title: Iran admits to Trump advisers "they made a mistake" in shooting at ships in strait
item.description: The White House wants Iran to publicly acknowledge that shooting at the ships was a mistake.

item.title: Apache pilots' suspensions lifted after flyby over South Carolina beach
item.description: The decision to suspend the pilots quickly led to backlash online and drew the attention of Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth.

item.title: Several killed in small plane crash in the Bahamas, officials say
item.description: A small plane crashed in the Bahamas on Friday, killing several people and prompting the government to temporarily ground Flamingo Air flights.

item.title: 7/10: CBS Evening News
item.description: Millions are under threat of dangerous flooding; a Mexican man was stopped by ICE officers before he was fatally shot.

item.title: Iran told Trump advisers it "made a mistake" in shooting at ships
item.description: Iran told the Trump administration privately that it "made a mistake" in shooting at commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz, senior U.S. officials said. The attack on three ships led to two days of intense strikes. CBS News political reporter Zak Hudak has more.

item.title: Curtis Sliwa says "it's time for Bibi to go" after poll finds Jewish Americans favor Mamdani more
item.description: A new poll from The Associated Press and the National Opinion Research Center finds that New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani has a higher favorability rating among Jewish Americans than Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu. Curtis Sliwa, who faced off against Mamdani as the Republican mayoral candidate, joins with his reaction.

item.title: Key takeaways from accused Charlie Kirk killer's weeklong pre-trial hearing
item.description: The weeklong pre-trial hearing for the man accused of killing conservative activist Charlie Kirk wrapped up on Friday with the defense calling one final witness to the stand. CBS News legal contributor Jessica Levinson joins with analysis.

item.title: Landmark bipartisan housing bill to become law without Trump's signature
item.description: The landmark bipartisan housing bill is set to become law overnight into Saturday despite President Trump's refusal to sign it. CBS News chief White House correspondent Nancy Cordes reports.

item.title: Astrophysicist Avi Loeb on Pentagon's 4th UFO files release
item.description: The Pentagon released more UFO files on Friday, its fourth tranche since May. Avi Loeb, Baird professor of science at Harvard University and leader of the White House UAP council, joins with analysis.

item.title: Trump administration to support heavy tariffs on Russian oil in bid to end Ukraine war, sources say
item.description: In the effort to end the Russia-Ukraine war, a bipartisan group of senators says the Trump administration is on board with legislation to impose more sanctions on Russia. CBS News congressional reporter Taurean Small has the details.

item.title: Charlie Kirk's family cites "overwhelming evidence" against alleged killer
item.description: Charlie Kirk's family said the end of Tyler Robinson's preliminary hearing "marks an important step forward in the pursuit of justice."
